summary: DeVault makes a systematic case for Alpine Linux as the best Linux distribution across all use cases, evaluating it against his core criteria of simplicity, stability, reliability, and robustness. He argues Alpine is uniquely simple among Linux distributions — the only one that 'fits in his head' — while also excelling at performance and access to up-to-date software. He acknowledges weaknesses in documentation and project governance but considers them minor relative to Alpine's strengths. He concludes by noting he uses Alpine everywhere: servers, workstations, laptops, and his PinePhone.
key_points: Alpine's simplicity is unmatched among Linux distributions, aided by choices like musl libc that reduce system complexity
The apk package manager is the fastest available and maintains correct, consistent system state even under pathological conditions
Alpine's six-month stable release cycle with two-year support strikes the right balance between stability and avoiding system atrophy
Documentation remains Alpine's weakest point, though simplicity partially compensates for this gap
Alpine's governance has issues with weak leadership and occasional political infighting, though this rarely impedes work
money_quotes: Alpine is the only Linux distribution that fits in my head.
An outdated system is not a robust system.
It expresses your constraints, and the constraints of your desired software, and solves for a system state which is always correct and consistent.
In these respects, Alpine is unmatched, and I would never dream of using any other Linux distribution in production.
apk is simply the fastest package manager available. It leaves apt and dnf in the dust, and is significantly faster than pacman.
Alpine's behavior under pathological conditions is generally predictable and easily understood.
This is generally offset by Alpine's simplicity — it can usually be understood reasonably quickly and easily even in the absence of documentation.
key_insight: Alpine Linux's greatest strength is being simple enough for one person to fully understand, which compounds into reliability, predictability, and robustness across every use case.
